extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE attacked 1.4 miles from finish on final Cat 3 climb, held off charging GC group including ROGLIČ and POGAČAR for stage win and yellow
tactical_patterns: VAN DER POEL positioned deliberately at rear of peloton early to avoid crash stress, contrasting with favorites riding front.
ROGLIČ bridged ALAPHILIPPE's solo move to contest stage bonuses, signaling aggressive GC seconds-hunting from opening stage.
Deceuninck team strength in final miles enabled ALAPHILIPPE's early 2.2km solo attack to hold despite GC contenders' chase.
ALAPHILIPPE crashed early but still had legs for winning move, suggesting early-stage crashes don't eliminate strong finishers on hilly stages.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Won from 2.2km solo attack after early crash, suggesting exceptional form and crash resilience
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: neutral: Deliberately rode at rear early to avoid stress, then positioned purple-yellow jersey near front for finale
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Aggressively chased ALAPHILIPPE for bonus seconds despite stage win unlikely, prioritizing every second from day one
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: neutral: Covered ROGLIČ's move but couldn't contest finale, showing GC awareness but not stage-winning form
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: Finished second after strong positioning through finale, handled Cat 3 climb better than pure sprinters
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Belgian champion jersey visible near front on finale climb, strong positioning despite not contesting finish
rider_signals.COLBRELLI Sonny: positive: Italian champion positioned strongly fourth in line on approach, handled climb well for punchy finisher
